metadata.source: Nepal Tourism Board, Pokhara Tourism Council, Official Tourism Websites
metadata.data_collection_note: All data collected from publicly available official tourism sources. Public tourist information only.
metadata.note: Tourist attractions and services in Pokhara

tourism.name: Phewa Lake
tourism.description: Second largest lake in Nepal, famous for boating and Tal Barahi Temple

tourism.name: World Peace Pagoda
tourism.description: Buddhist stupa with panoramic views of Annapurna range and Phewa Lake

tourism.name: Sarangkot
tourism.description: Famous sunrise viewpoint with Himalayan panorama and paragliding

tourism.name: Devi's Fall (Patale Chhango)
tourism.description: Waterfall that flows into an underground tunnel

tourism.name: Gupteshwor Mahadev Cave
tourism.description: Sacred cave with limestone formations and waterfall view

tourism.name: International Mountain Museum
tourism.description: Museum dedicated to mountains, mountaineering and Himalayan culture

tourism.name: Bindhyabasini Temple
tourism.description: Ancient temple dedicated to Goddess Bhagwati

tourism.name: Seti River Gorge
tourism.description: Deep gorge carved by Seti River with white water

tourism.name: Tal Barahi Temple
tourism.description: Two-story pagoda temple dedicated to Goddess Barahi

tourism.name: Davis Falls (Patale Chhango)
tourism.description: Famous waterfall named after a Swiss tourist

tourism.name: Begnas Lake
tourism.description: Third largest lake in Nepal, popular for fishing and boating

tourism.name: Pokhara Airport
tourism.description: Regional airport connecting to Kathmandu and Jomsom
